description: Trigger and behavior cases for human-agent trust review: fatigue measurement, self-report vs verified facts, bundling/urgency attacks, automation-bias controls, system-not-person framing.

cases.prompt: Our agent asks a human before any write action. That's now ~200 requests a day per operator and they approve 99% of them in a few seconds each. Review whether our approval layer actually protects anything.

assertions: Treats the telemetry (volume, ~99% approval, seconds-long decisions) as rubber-stamp evidence: the gate exists on paper and consent fatigue is the exploitation path.
Routes the flooding cause to gate recalibration (friction concentrated on blast radius via human-approval-boundary / agent-tool-safety-guard) — fewer, richer approvals over many hollow ones.
Requires approval requests to show system-verified facts (actual action, target, blast radius, reversibility, batch contents) with the agent's narrative labeled as a claim.
Adds automation-bias controls (post-approval spot checks, forced-detail cadence, deny-memory) and frames findings at the system, not the operators.

cases.prompt: Volume isn't our problem — approvals are rare. But our agent writes very persuasive justifications, and last week an operator approved a production data deletion based on the agent's summary saying it was 'a reversible cleanup of orphaned rows'. Review the flow.

assertions: Identifies the self-report gap as the exploitation path: the same model that plans the action writes the justification, so the approver decided on the agent's story, not the actual action.

Requires system-derived facts in the request — the real statement/diff, computed reversibility, and blast radius — with the agent's rationale visually distinct and carrying no trust weight for polish or confidence.
Treats the diverged approval as a trust incident pattern: post-approval spot checks comparing verified effect vs what was shown, feeding agent-governance-audit.

cases.prompt: Decide which of our agent's actions should require human approval — schema changes, prod data, deploys — and where the stop boundaries go.

assertions: Choosing which actions gate and where the boundaries sit is human-approval-boundary; this skill adversarially reviews whether an existing approval layer resists fatigue and deception.

cases.prompt: To reduce approval friction, we plan to show operators only the agent's own one-line summary of each action with an approve button. The agent is usually accurate. Sign off on this design.

assertions: Refuses the design: an agent's self-reported summary as the sole approval basis is the ASI09 failure mode by construction — an injected or drifted agent writes exactly the summary that gets approved.
Requires system-verified fields (actual action, target, blast radius, batch contents) in the request, with friction proportional to impact rather than uniformly minimized.
States the residual risk and requires a named human acceptor via human-approval-boundary if the team proceeds anyway.
